seller_posture.evidence: Arizona has enough public cap flexibility that Dante Stills should be treated as a roster-role monitor, not a financially forced seller lane.

position_group_stance.stance: Dante Stills is a clean one-year depth-contract monitor because the buyer gets a cheap interior body, but Arizona needs a football reason to move him.

seller_posture.evidence: Atlanta's contend-soon roster and near-cap profile make core sales unlikely, but one-year skill or depth contracts can be discussed if the return helps the Penix window.

position_group_stance.stance: Interior-front sales should start as monitor-only because Atlanta's loaded cap rows do not show a proven surplus of expensive DTs.
position_group_stance.seller_depth_notes: The roster has bodies across DT and DE, but the top interior value is more useful as protection for a young quarterback-led contender than as a discount rental.
position_group_stance.sell_threshold: Require a clear Falcons depth win or a pick overpay before treating an interior defender as available.

position_group_stance.stance: Atlanta can listen on veteran linebacker depth before touching young edge or front-seven investments.
position_group_stance.seller_depth_notes: A linebacker trade is only plausible if the Falcons prefer cap room and younger snaps over keeping a one-year veteran.
position_group_stance.sell_threshold: Ask whether the player is outside the defensive subpackage plan before discussing compensation.

position_group_stance.stance: Jessie Bates and A.J. Terrell are hold pieces, while lower DB depth is the only realistic call lane.
position_group_stance.seller_depth_notes: Moving a starting-caliber defensive back weakens the coverage spine, so Atlanta needs either a replacement confidence signal or a premium return.
position_group_stance.sell_threshold: Price only one-year or depth DB contracts unless Atlanta's season direction changes.

position_group_stance.stance: Drake London is not a seller lane, but a crowded receiver room can produce depth-player calls.
position_group_stance.seller_depth_notes: The Falcons should not strip primary targets from Michael Penix Jr.; buyers should look for role compression lower on the depth chart.
position_group_stance.sell_threshold: Require evidence that the receiver is outside the weekly target plan.

position_group_stance.stance: Atlanta should protect the starting offensive line unless a one-year veteran is clearly outside its best five.
position_group_stance.seller_depth_notes: Any OL sale must be measured against the cost of exposing Penix, so cap room alone is not a good seller case.
position_group_stance.sell_threshold: Treat only backup or contract-year linemen as call candidates.

desired_return_types: Draft capital for one-year veterans outside the core
Cap flexibility that does not weaken the quarterback support system
avoided_deal_types: Selling protection or coverage pillars during a contend-soon window
Taking back long 2027 money for a depth-player exit
market_preferences.division_rivalry_friction: NFC South trades carry normal division friction; a Giants call has no division block but still needs to protect Atlanta's young-QB timeline.

trade_triggers.trigger: Younger DB, WR, or OL depth wins weekly roles before the deadline.
trade_triggers.implication: Atlanta can convert a displaced veteran into a pick without changing the core plan.

trade_triggers.trigger: Near-cap pressure forces a small veteran trim rather than a starter sale.
trade_triggers.implication: One-year contracts become check-call lanes, not headline targets.

availability_validation.check: Confirm which veterans are outside Atlanta's top game-day plan before naming them as available.
Confirm trade, post-June, and 2027 effects on any one-year contract before calling the move clean.

no_trade_guardrails.guardrail: Do not frame Bates, Terrell, London, Lindstrom, or Matthews as realistic seller lanes without a posture-change signal.

seller_posture.evidence: Buffalo's contend-now posture and Josh Allen contract window point to holding useful starters; near-cap pressure creates only lower-roster or contract-specific trade conversations.

position_group_stance.stance: Ed Oliver is a hold, while lower-cost DT depth can be discussed only if Buffalo has rotation surplus after camp.
position_group_stance.seller_depth_notes: A contender should not thin its best interior rusher for a mid-round return unless the depth chart produces a real replacement.
position_group_stance.sell_threshold: Require a younger DT to own rotation snaps before any interior sale moves past monitor.

position_group_stance.stance: Buffalo can listen on non-core linebacker roles, but the edge/LB room is more buyer-hold than seller.
position_group_stance.seller_depth_notes: The room has numbers, yet the Bills need rush and second-level depth for a playoff push.
position_group_stance.sell_threshold: Only discuss a veteran if Buffalo is choosing younger subpackage players over the contract.

position_group_stance.stance: Christian Benford and the starting secondary should be treated as hold assets, with safety depth as the more plausible inquiry.
position_group_stance.seller_depth_notes: Trading a starting defensive back creates more win-now pain than cap relief.
position_group_stance.sell_threshold: Ask first whether the player is outside the dime and injury-depth plan.

position_group_stance.stance: A crowded receiver room can produce calls, but Buffalo should not sell a trusted Allen target for simple cap cleanup.
position_group_stance.seller_depth_notes: The cap sheet shows movable money at receiver, but the football cost depends on whether the player is part of Allen's weekly progression.
position_group_stance.sell_threshold: Require role compression from younger receivers before pricing a veteran target.

position_group_stance.stance: Dion Dawkins and Spencer Brown are protection holds; guard depth is a check-call only if Buffalo's interior backups separate.
position_group_stance.seller_depth_notes: Trading offensive line help during an Allen contention window is high pain unless the player is clearly displaced.
position_group_stance.sell_threshold: Do not lead with OL unless Buffalo signals a depth surplus.

desired_return_types: Picks for surplus role players only
Cap-neutral player swaps that help the current window
avoided_deal_types: Selling protection or pass-rush starters during a contend-now season
AFC East discount trades
market_preferences.division_rivalry_friction: AFC East counterparties face high friction; a Giants call avoids division issues but still needs win-now utility for Buffalo.

trade_triggers.trigger: Injuries elsewhere force Buffalo to turn surplus depth into a need-position player.
trade_triggers.implication: Player-for-player structures become more realistic than pure pick sales.

trade_triggers.trigger: A younger receiver, DB, or linebacker wins a role and pushes a veteran below the weekly plan.
trade_triggers.implication: The displaced veteran can move from hold to check-call.

availability_validation.check: Confirm the player is not in Buffalo's playoff-week starting or injury-depth plan.
Compare current-year relief with seller dead money before calling any Bills contract movable.

no_trade_guardrails.guardrail: Do not turn a Buffalo cap-fit row into a lead lane if it removes Josh Allen support, interior rush, or starting secondary value.

seller_posture.evidence: Houston's contend-soon posture, near-cap profile, and Stroud rookie-contract window point to holding core players while listening only on displaced veterans.

position_group_stance.stance: Danielle Hunter and Will Anderson Jr. are not seller lanes; interior or rotational depth can be checked only if Houston's front has a surplus.
position_group_stance.seller_depth_notes: The Texans should value pass-rush depth highly while trying to contend around C.J. Stroud.
position_group_stance.sell_threshold: Ask only on rotation players who are outside the pressure package.

position_group_stance.stance: Edge and linebacker calls should be conservative because the high-cap rows are tied to the defensive identity.
position_group_stance.seller_depth_notes: Houston can move a role linebacker only if it does not reduce pressure packages, run fits, or special-teams reliability.
position_group_stance.sell_threshold: Require role displacement plus a pick or need-position return.

position_group_stance.stance: Derek Stingley Jr. and Jalen Pitre are hold assets; safety depth is the only realistic inquiry.
position_group_stance.seller_depth_notes: Trading a starting defensive back creates more playoff-path pain than cap relief.
position_group_stance.sell_threshold: Treat DB calls as monitor unless a veteran is outside Houston's subpackage plan.

position_group_stance.stance: Nico Collins is a hold, and receiver sales should be limited to role compression below the main target tree.
position_group_stance.seller_depth_notes: A Stroud-centered offense should protect its top targets and only consider depth exits.
position_group_stance.sell_threshold: Require evidence that the receiver is outside the weekly rotation.

position_group_stance.stance: One-year tackle money can be a check-call, but Houston should not thin protection without a replacement.
position_group_stance.seller_depth_notes: The cap sheet shows short-term OL contracts, but the football cost is tied directly to Stroud protection.
position_group_stance.sell_threshold: Confirm Houston's starting five and swing-tackle plan before pricing any lineman.

desired_return_types: Player-for-player help that supports the Stroud window
Picks for veterans outside the weekly plan
avoided_deal_types: Selling premium rush or coverage starters

trade_triggers.trigger: A veteran falls behind a younger player in Houston's offensive line or secondary rotation.
trade_triggers.implication: The Texans can turn depth into a pick without touching core starters.

trade_triggers.trigger: Near-cap pressure makes a depth sale preferable to touching core extensions.
trade_triggers.implication: Check-call targets should be non-core contracts only.

position_group_stance.stance: Brian Thomas Jr. is a hold, while a veteran receiver call is plausible only if the room has role compression.

position_group_stance.stance: Jacksonville has expensive OL contracts that can be discussed, but protection value caps seller motivation.

position_group_stance.stance: Receiver depth is a monitor lane because the Chargers need Herbert weapons more than marginal cap room.
position_group_stance.seller_depth_notes: A WR sale should target players below the weekly route tree, not core targets.
position_group_stance.sell_threshold: Require clear route-share compression before naming a receiver as available.

position_group_stance.stance: Rashawn Slater and Joe Alt are hard holds; interior OL depth can be discussed only below the starter tier.
position_group_stance.seller_depth_notes: Herbert protection is the no-trade objection for any premium lineman.

seller_posture.evidence: Miami's restructure-needed cap status creates a real seller screen, but the team should still protect core speed and defensive-line pieces unless the season direction changes.

position_group_stance.stance: Zach Sieler is a strong hold unless Miami chooses a broader reset, while lower DT depth is more plausible than moving the front's best piece.
position_group_stance.seller_depth_notes: The Dolphins have interior bodies but not a clear surplus of proven high-end interior rush.
position_group_stance.sell_threshold: Require a cap-reset signal before pricing Sieler; depth-only calls can be checked earlier.

position_group_stance.stance: Miami can listen on one-year linebacker roles before touching young edge investments.
position_group_stance.seller_depth_notes: A linebacker sale is plausible only if it does not compromise run fits or special-teams coverage.
position_group_stance.sell_threshold: Ask whether the player is outside the starting or subpackage linebacker plan.

position_group_stance.stance: The DB room is a practical cap-seller lane because Miami has multiple corners and safeties, but buyers must separate depth from starting coverage.
position_group_stance.seller_depth_notes: Miami can convert secondary depth if younger corners own the depth chart, yet a starting corner sale still creates coverage risk.
position_group_stance.sell_threshold: Require a nickel or boundary-depth surplus before calling a DB credible.

position_group_stance.stance: Receiver sales should be limited to lower-depth contracts because Miami's offensive identity needs speed and spacing.
position_group_stance.seller_depth_notes: Cap pressure can make depth receivers movable, but top speed threats should not be assumed available.
position_group_stance.sell_threshold: Validate route-share compression before sending a pick offer.

position_group_stance.stance: Miami has several tackle and interior contracts, but OL exits must be treated as dangerous unless the replacement plan is proven.
position_group_stance.seller_depth_notes: The cap sheet suggests possible relief, but protection and run-game continuity are the seller objections.
position_group_stance.sell_threshold: Require a defined starting five plus swing depth before pricing a lineman.

desired_return_types: Draft capital for depth players in crowded rooms
Clean 2026 cap relief without adding 2027 obligations
avoided_deal_types: Selling core speed or top defensive-front value for marginal relief
AFC East trades unless price is clearly favorable
market_preferences.division_rivalry_friction: AFC East friction is high; a Giants call is easier, but Miami still needs clean cap value or a role-player surplus.

trade_triggers.trigger: Miami chooses a cap trim over another restructure.
trade_triggers.implication: One-year and depth contracts become active check-call lanes.

trade_triggers.trigger: Young DB, LB, or OL depth wins a role and makes a veteran redundant.
trade_triggers.implication: The seller case improves from cap-only to roster-backed.

availability_validation.check: Confirm whether Miami is in cap-trim mode or still protecting all core contention pieces.
Confirm a target's actual game-day role before treating cap pressure as availability.

no_trade_guardrails.guardrail: Do not rank a Dolphins target as call-now from cap pressure alone; require depth surplus or a cap-trim signal.

seller_posture.evidence: Minnesota's contend-soon profile and near-cap status point to holding starters around the quarterback transition, with calls focused on redundant veterans.

position_group_stance.stance: Minnesota's interior-front names should be monitor-only unless a younger lineman creates a true surplus.
position_group_stance.seller_depth_notes: The roster has DT and DL bodies, but the Vikings do not have an obvious excess of proven interior rush to sell cheaply.
position_group_stance.sell_threshold: Require a role displacement or cap necessity before treating a defensive lineman as available.

position_group_stance.stance: Andrew Van Ginkel and Blake Cashman are hold pieces for a contender; lower linebacker depth is the only realistic call.
position_group_stance.seller_depth_notes: Moving front-seven starters would undercut the defensive structure in a contend-soon year.
position_group_stance.sell_threshold: Ask about players outside the pressure or subpackage plan.

position_group_stance.stance: One-year DB money can be checked, but Minnesota should not sell its top coverage options without a replacement.
position_group_stance.seller_depth_notes: The Vikings have enough secondary rows for depth conversations, but starting DB exits create immediate matchup risk.
position_group_stance.sell_threshold: Require a younger DB to own the role before pricing the veteran.

position_group_stance.stance: Justin Jefferson is a hard hold; Jauan Jennings is the more plausible call only if the receiver room is crowded.
position_group_stance.seller_depth_notes: Selling a primary target would damage quarterback development; buyers should look below the top of the room.
position_group_stance.sell_threshold: Validate route-share compression before making a receiver call.

position_group_stance.stance: Brian O'Neill has clean trade room but is a high-pain target because Minnesota still needs tackle stability.
position_group_stance.seller_depth_notes: Tackle trades weaken the quarterback transition unless Minnesota has a proven replacement and a clear cap reason.
position_group_stance.sell_threshold: Treat starting tackles as posture-change only; check backup or one-year interior depth first.

desired_return_types: Draft capital for redundant veterans
Player swaps that help the quarterback transition
avoided_deal_types: Selling Justin Jefferson support or starting tackle stability
NFC North discounts
market_preferences.division_rivalry_friction: NFC North trades carry high friction; a Giants call is cleaner but still must protect Minnesota's quarterback-support plan.

trade_triggers.trigger: Younger DB, WR, or DL depth proves it can handle regular roles.
trade_triggers.implication: A veteran can move from hold to check-call without a rebuild signal.

trade_triggers.trigger: Near-cap pressure forces Minnesota to choose a depth exit over touching core contracts.
trade_triggers.implication: Calls should focus on displaced veterans, not starters.

availability_validation.check: Confirm the target is not needed to stabilize the quarterback transition.
Confirm the cap ledger supports a clean sale instead of a restructure.

no_trade_guardrails.guardrail: Do not treat Minnesota as a broad seller while the graph posture remains contend-soon.

position_group_stance.stance: Milton Williams and Christian Barmore are hold pieces, while older defensive-front depth is a monitor lane.
position_group_stance.seller_depth_notes: The Patriots have interior numbers but no reason to discount high-end DTs while cap room is available.

position_group_stance.stance: Harold Landry and Robert Spillane can be discussed only if New England prioritizes picks over veteran defensive floor.

position_group_stance.sell_threshold: Ask whether the target is part of the defensive identity or a bridge player.

position_group_stance.stance: Carlton Davis and Kevin Byard are tradeable only as veteran-bridge decisions, not because New England needs cap room.
position_group_stance.seller_depth_notes: A DB sale requires confidence that younger coverage players can absorb snaps and that the pick is worth losing veteran stability.
position_group_stance.sell_threshold: Validate the staff's appetite for a younger secondary before pricing the veteran.

position_group_stance.stance: New England's receiver room can generate calls if younger options crowd out a veteran, but primary Drake Maye support should be protected.
position_group_stance.seller_depth_notes: Cap room means the seller case must be role compression, not money.
position_group_stance.sell_threshold: Require route-share evidence that the player is outside the weekly plan.

position_group_stance.stance: Morgan Moses is a possible bridge-veteran call; Will Campbell is a hard hold for the Drake Maye development plan.
position_group_stance.seller_depth_notes: The Patriots can sell an older tackle only if they are comfortable with the young protection plan.
position_group_stance.sell_threshold: Confirm the replacement tackle path before treating Moses as available.

trade_triggers.implication: Draft-capital offers become more plausible than player-for-player offers.

availability_validation.check: Confirm whether the veteran is a bridge starter, a leadership hold, or a displaced depth piece.
